extractedFieldValues.current_value: Leon Coburn, an 84-year-old male, has been referred for home health care primarily due to his diagnosis of bilateral shoulder osteoarthritis, accompanied by a history of chronic low back pain, hypertension, hypercholesterolemia, and atrial fibrillation. His recent medical evaluation indicated significant functional limitations, particularly in the range of motion of both shoulders, which is critical for daily activities such as dressing and grooming. The patient has reported pain and difficulty moving his arms, which was exacerbated following a recent cortisone injection in the left shoulder. This condition warrants home health services to support his recovery and management of symptoms.
source_spans: Primary Reason for Home Health/Medical Necessity (POC Element):

Leon Coburn, an 84-year-old male, has been referred for home health care primarily due to his diagnosis of bilateral shoulder osteoarthritis, accompanied by a history of chronic low back pain, hypertension, hypercholesterolemia, and atrial fibrillation.

extractedFieldValues.current_value: Patient is not Homebound Criteria One (One must be met): Describe what assistive devices the patient is dependent on (if any) and specifically why the patient condition warrants the special transportation or the assistance of another person or describe the condition that makes leaving home medically contraindicated. Criteria 1 A: Because of illness or injury Patient needs the aid of supportive devices Crutches Cane Manual Wheel Chair Walker Motorized Wheelchair Scooter Patient Needs the use of special transportation. Patient needs the assistance of another person to leave the place of residence Criteria 1 B: Has a condition such as leaving his or her home is medically contraindicated. Status post surgery Angina upon minimal exertion Immuno deficiency Other Homebound Reason : Assistance needed for safe mobility and transfer Criteria Two (Both must be met) Criteria 2 A: There must be exist a normal inability to leave home. Dyspnea on minimal exertion Bedbound Chair bound Dementia Blindness Angina upon minimal exertion Paralysis Quadprilegic or Paraplegic or Hemiplegia Unable to tolerate standing or walking for extended periods Weakness and pain from recent surgery Experiencing pain impairs/restricts mobility Visually impaired-requires constant attendance/assist Balance impaired with potential for falls; uses walls and furniture to ambulate Cognitively impaired, unable to safely leave home without assist Leaving home is exhausting, extended rest periods to recover Infectious disease process causing weakness and exhaustion Extended respiratory recovery after exertion Narcotics impaired ability to safely leave home unassisted Requires human assist due to unsteady gait/poor balance Bedbound and requires EMS support Other Homebound Reason : High risk for fall and injury Criteria 2 B: Leaving home must require a considerable and taxing effort F_CTL_69ceccf5e5698b0c6bd4314b_0 COBURN, LEON I 178822 I DOB: 12/04/1941 I N: 04/03/2026 2 of 19 Special transportation Requires assistive device to ambulate Severe pain on ambulation Exhausting to navigate vehicle and require human assist Leaving familiar environment causes fear and confusion Requires assist to manage equipment and public transportation Leaving home is strenous requiring extended rest to recover Needs assistance to handle and transport equipment Fatigue to arrange/use appropriate transportation Activities restricted due to pain limitations Other Homebound Reason : Prognosis (POC Element ($484.60 (2.v))): 1-Poor 2-Guarded 3-Fair 4-Good 5-Excellent Functional Limitations (POC Element ($484.60 (2.vi))): 1 - Amputation 4 Hearing 7 - Ambulation A - Dyspnea with minimal exertion 2- Bowel/Bladder (Incontinence) 5 - Paralysis 8 - Speech 3 - Contracture 6 - Endurance 9 - Legally blind B- Other (Specify) Emergency Preparedness (POC Element):
